html:
The Pan Flute is a Magic item. It requires 5 Cut Reeds, 1 Mandrake, and 1 Rope to craft, and a Prestihatitator to prototype.

When played, it puts all Mobs within 3.75 Tiles to sleep, except for those that are incapable of sleeping.

It takes 1 second to begin playing the Pan Flute after clicking it. Mobs will be put into a deeper sleep than usual, allowing the player to move closer to them without waking them up; for example, the Tallbird.

If a mob that was put to sleep is attacked, all of the similar nearby mobs will wake up after a couple of seconds. Mobs sometimes sleep all day after they have been put to sleep by the Pan Flute.

The Pan Flute can be used to get an advantage over enemies, shave Beefalo, or steal Tallbird Eggs and Pengull Eggs, to cool down enraged Dragonfly and buy time against Toadstool when it is trying to summon Sporecaps. The Pan Flute also complements the Feather Hat when looking for Morsels and Feathers.

Mandrakes are rare and if the player wishes to use Pan Flute more, they can deconstruct a Pan Flute using a Deconstruction Staff and craft it again to renew its durability. Mandrakes also have a chance to be obtained from Klaus's loot.


Downloadable Content

In the Reign of Giants DLC, the Pan Flute has a 75% chance to be found around the Glommer's Statue.

In the Shipwrecked DLC, the Pan Flute can rarely be found when fishing with the Trawl Net, making it renewable.

In the Hamlet DLC, the Pan Flute is renewable since Mandrakes can be bought in 'The Sty' Oddities Emporium for 50 Oincs.

Trivia

  • Pan Flutes are named after Pan, the Greek god of animals and music.
  • The Pan Flute is one of the 6 ways to put mobs to sleep, with the others methods being the Sleep Dart, Life Giving Amulet, Wickerbottom's book Sleepytime Stories, and eating or cooking a Mandrake.
  • Using a Pan Flute near a Bird in flight will cause it to fall to the ground, ready to be picked up for a short time.
  • It is also apparently Wortox's flute, according to his character quote. This would make sense as the Pan Flute is shown to be shadow magic, and Wortox is an imp, which, according to folklore, dabble in the dark arts.
    • It is also shown in the portrait for his Verdant skin, which has similarities to Satyrs, who would use Pan Flutes in folk tales.
  • Making a Pan Flute out of a Mandrake is a much more efficient use of a Mandrake, as cooking and eating Mandrakes only have two uses, destroy the Mandrake, and also put the user to sleep. Pan Flutes have ten uses, but if used only nine times can be renewed with Deconstruction Staffs, and do not put the user to sleep.

Gallery

  • Original HD icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.
  • Wilson playing the Pan Flute.
  • Maxwell putting Hounds to sleep.
Tools and WeaponsCraftable Items

The Pan Flute is a Magic item. It requires 5 Cut Reeds, 1 Mandrake, and 1 Rope to craft, and a Prestihatitator to prototype.
When played, it puts all Mobs within 3.75 Tiles to sleep, except for those that are incapable of sleeping.
It takes 1 second to begin playing the Pan Flute after clicking it. Mobs will be put into a deeper sleep than usual, allowing the player to move closer to them without waking them up; for example, the Tallbird.
If a mob that was put to sleep is attacked, all of the similar nearby mobs will wake up after a couple of seconds. Mobs sometimes sleep all day after they have been put to sleep by the Pan Flute.
The Pan Flute can be used to get an advantage over enemies, shave Beefalo, or steal Tallbird Eggs and Pengull Eggs, to cool down enraged Dragonfly and buy time against Toadstool when it is trying to summon Sporecaps. The Pan Flute also complements the Feather Hat when looking for Morsels and Feathers.
Mandrakes are rare and if the player wishes to use Pan Flute more, they can deconstruct a Pan Flute using a Deconstruction Staff and craft it again to renew its durability. Mandrakes also have a chance to be obtained from Klaus's loot.
Downloadable Content[edit | edit source]
In the Reign of Giants DLC, the Pan Flute has a 75% chance to be found around the Glommer's Statue.
In the Shipwrecked DLC, the Pan Flute can rarely be found when fishing with the Trawl Net, making it renewable.
In the Hamlet DLC, the Pan Flute is renewable since Mandrakes can be bought in 'The Sty' Oddities Emporium for 50 Oincs.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
Pan Flutes are named after Pan, the Greek god of animals and music.
The Pan Flute is one of the 6 ways to put mobs to sleep, with the others methods being the Sleep Dart, Life Giving Amulet, Wickerbottom's book Sleepytime Stories, and eating or cooking a Mandrake.
Using a Pan Flute near a Bird in flight will cause it to fall to the ground, ready to be picked up for a short time.
It is also apparently Wortox's flute, according to his character quote. This would make sense as the Pan Flute is shown to be shadow magic, and Wortox is an imp, which, according to folklore, dabble in the dark arts.
It is also shown in the portrait for his Verdant skin, which has similarities to Satyrs, who would use Pan Flutes in folk tales.
Making a Pan Flute out of a Mandrake is a much more efficient use of a Mandrake, as cooking and eating Mandrakes only have two uses, destroy the Mandrake, and also put the user to sleep. Pan Flutes have ten uses, but if used only nine times can be renewed with Deconstruction Staffs, and do not put the user to sleep.

Sáo Pan là một vật phẩm Phép thuật. Nó cần 5 Sậy Cắt, 1 Nhân Sâm, và 1 Dây Thừng để chế tạo, và một Máy Mũ Cao Cấp để nguyên mẫu.

The '''Pan Flute''' is a [[Magic]] [[item]]. It requires 5 [[Cut Reeds]], 1 [[Mandrake]], and 1 [[Rope]] to craft, and a [[Prestihatitator]] to prototype.

When played, it puts all [[Mobs]] within 3.75 [[Tile]]s to [[sleep]], except for those that are incapable of sleeping.

It takes 1 second to begin playing the Pan Flute after clicking it. Mobs will be put into a deeper sleep than usual, allowing the player to move closer to them without waking them up; for example, the [[Tallbird]].

If a mob that was put to sleep is attacked, all of the similar nearby mobs will wake up after a couple of seconds. Mobs sometimes sleep all day after they have been put to sleep by the Pan Flute.

The Pan Flute can be used to get an advantage over enemies, shave [[Beefalo]], or steal [[Tallbird Egg]]s and [[Pengull]] [[Eggs]], to cool down enraged [[Dragonfly]]  and buy time against [[Toadstool]] when it is trying to summon Sporecaps. The Pan Flute also complements the [[Feather Hat]] when looking for [[Morsel]]s and [[Feather]]s.

[[Mandrake]]s are rare and if the player wishes to use Pan Flute more, they can deconstruct a Pan Flute using a [[Deconstruction Staff]] and craft it again to renew its durability. Mandrakes also have a chance to be obtained from [[Klaus]]'s loot.

__NOTOC__
=={{Pic32|Gift Icon}} Downloadable Content==
In the ''[[Don't Starve: Reign of Giants|Reign of Giants]]'' DLC, the Pan Flute has a 75% chance to be found around the [[Glommer's Statue]]. 

In the ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked|Shipwrecked]]'' DLC, the Pan Flute can rarely be found when fishing with the [[Trawl Net]], making it renewable.

In the ''[[Hamlet]]'' DLC, the Pan Flute is renewable since [[Mandrake]]s can be bought in [['The Sty' Oddities Emporium]] for 50 [[Oinc]]s.

=={{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia==
* Pan Flutes are named after [[wikipedia:Pan_(god)|Pan]], the Greek god of animals and music.
* The Pan Flute is one of the 6 ways to put mobs to sleep, with the others methods being the [[Sleep Dart]], [[Life Giving Amulet]], [[Wickerbottom|Wickerbottom's]] book [[Sleepytime Stories]], and eating or cooking a Mandrake.
* Using a Pan Flute near a [[Birds|Bird]] in flight will cause it to fall to the ground, ready to be picked up for a short time.
*It is also apparently [[Wortox]]'s flute, according to his character quote. This would make sense as the Pan Flute is shown to be [[Magic Filter|shadow magic]], and Wortox is an imp, which, according to folklore, dabble in the dark arts.
**It is also shown in the portrait for his Verdant skin, which has similarities to Satyrs, who would use Pan Flutes in folk tales.
*Making a Pan Flute out of a Mandrake is a much more efficient use of a Mandrake, as cooking and eating Mandrakes only have two uses, destroy the Mandrake, and also put the user to sleep. Pan Flutes have ten uses, but if used only nine times can be renewed with Deconstruction Staffs, and do not put the user to sleep.
